Accessibility Findings

https://i94.cbp.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 5

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus)

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,384 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 138,444 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,323 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 138,444 affected page loads) ~415People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~415 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 138,444 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~3,046 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 138,444 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: body.mat-typography > i94-root > mat-drawer-container.mat-drawer-container > div.cdk-visually-hidden

Snippet:

<div tabindex="0" class="cdk-visually-hidden cdk-focus-trap-anchor" aria-hidden="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

Element 2

Element path: body.mat-typography > i94-root > mat-drawer-container.mat-drawer-container > div.cdk-visually-hidden

Snippet:

<div tabindex="0" class="cdk-visually-hidden cdk-focus-trap-anchor" aria-hidden="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,384 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 138,444 affected page loads) ~415People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~3,046 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 138,444 affected page loads)

Label: Visiting the U.S. & Arriving Via a Land Border or Selected Ferry?

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Snippet:

<a href="https://vote.gov/" class="menu__link" data-ga="Footer:Vote.gov">

Label: Vote.gov

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (60.4px by 22px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 22px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://ustraveldocs.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 4

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,338 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 133,821 affected page loads) ~401People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~401 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 133,821 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,944 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 133,821 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.rich-text > div#choose-your-location-A > div.usa-space > h4.usa-accordion__heading

Snippet:

<h4 data-v-593890b4="" class="usa-accordion__heading">

Label: A

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport)

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,212 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 133,821 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="user-scalable=no,initial-scale=1,maximum-scale=1,minimum-scale=1,width=dev…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch)

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,334 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 133,444 affected page loads) ~400People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~400 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 133,444 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.top-nav-bar > div.top-nav-bar-bottom > div.left > img

Snippet:

<img src="https://www.fbi.gov/++theme++fbigov.theme/uswds-2.9.0/img/icon-dot-gov.svg">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2

Element path: div.top-nav-bar > div.top-nav-bar-bottom > div.right > img

Snippet:

<img src="https://www.fbi.gov/++theme++fbigov.theme/uswds-2.9.0/img/icon-https.svg">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,936 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 133,444 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch)

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Accessibility Findings

https://osha.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 83

Axe findings: 4

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,281 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 128,089 affected page loads) ~384People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~384 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 128,089 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div#hero-callout > p > a > img.align-center

Snippet:

<img aalt="OSHA Cares" src="/sites/default/files/inline-images/OSHA-Cares_logo.png" style="margin:0 auto; display: block; object-fit: initial;" width="400" class="align-center">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list)

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,281 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 128,089 affected page loads) ~384People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content.   • List element has direct children that are not allowed: div
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem)

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Accessibility Findings

https://mymarketnews.ams.usda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 83

Axe findings: 4

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus)

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,252 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,004 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads) ~376People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~376 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,754 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.flexslider > div.flex-viewport > ul.slides > li.clone

Snippet:

<li class="clone" aria-hidden="true" style="width: 352px; margin-right: 0px; float: left; display: block;">

Label: Contamination-Free Cotton AMS implements new extraneous matter code for plasti…

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

Element 2

Element path: div.flexslider > div.flex-viewport > ul.slides > li.clone

Snippet:

<li style="width: 352px; margin-right: 0px; float: left; display: block;" class="clone" aria-hidden="true">

Label: My Market News Resources Learn more about My Market News

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM
`[role]`s do not have all required `[aria-*]` attributes (rule: aria-required-attr)

Some ARIA roles have required attributes that describe the state of the element to screen readers. Learn more about roles and required attributes.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,252 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads) ~376People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~376 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,754 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: header#headerTop > div.container > div.row > div.col-sm-12

Snippet:

<div class="col-sm-12" role="heading">

Label: About AMS News and Announcements Careers For Employees Contact Us A-Z Glossary …

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA attribute not present: aria-level
Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,252 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads) ~376People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~376 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.form-item > div.input-group > span.input-group-btn > button.button

Snippet:

<button class="button js-form-submit form-submit btn-primary form-control icon-only" name="" type="submit" value="Search">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,004 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads) ~5KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~5,383 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 125,183 affected page loads)

Affected elements (4):

Element 1

Element path: section#block-aboutmarketnews > div.field > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="/Public%20Notices">

Label: view

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.51:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #337ab7, surrounding text: #5a5a5a)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 2

Element path: section#block-aboutmarketnews > div.field > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="/general-resources">

Label: view

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.51:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #337ab7, surrounding text: #5a5a5a)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 3

Element path: section#block-aboutmarketnews > div.field > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.ams.usda.gov/notices?field_date_value%5Bvalue%5D%5Byear%5D=&amp;ti…">

Label: view

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.51:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #337ab7, surrounding text: #5a5a5a)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 4

Element path: section#block-aboutmarketnews > div.field > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="/visualizations">

Label: view

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.51:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #337ab7, surrounding text: #5a5a5a)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children)

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent)

Accessibility Findings

https://federalregister.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 79

Axe findings: 6

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,239 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 123,920 affected page loads) ~372People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~372 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 123,920 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,726 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 123,920 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: main > div#main > div > div#suggestions

Snippet:

<div id="suggestions" class="" data-controller="suggestions autocomplete" data-autocomplete-url-value="/suggestions" data-autocomplete-delay-value="300" aria-expanded="false" data-autocomplete-ready-value="true">

Label:

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,974 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 123,920 affected page loads) ~5KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~5,329 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 123,920 affected page loads)

Element path: div.homepage-grid > div#embedded-search-area > form#embedded_entry_search_form > span#expected_result_count

Snippet:

<span id="expected_result_count" class="search-result-count">

Label: 995,940 documents

<span class="fr-doc-date">

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,239 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 123,920 affected page loads) ~372People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Element 1

Element path: div.fr-head-metadata-bar > div.fr-head-meta-label-holder > div.fr-head-meta-label > h4

Snippet:

<h4>

Label: Current Issue

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,974 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 123,920 affected page loads) ~5KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~5,329 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 123,920 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1

Element path: div.fr-head-meta-counts > ul.fr-list > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="/documents/search?conditions%5Bpublication_date%5D%5Bis%5D=2026-03-26">

Label: 92 documents

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.78:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #4d5e30, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 2

Element path: div.content-block > div#home-aid-tagline > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="/reader-aids">

Label: here

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.66:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #245784, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Tables do not use `<caption>` instead of cells with the `[colspan]` attribute to indicate a caption. (rule: table-fake-caption)

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that tables use the actual caption element instead of cells with the `[colspan]` attribute may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about captions.

Accessibility Findings

https://ipps-a.army.mil

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 83

Axe findings: 5

`button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements do not have accessible names. (rule: aria-command-name)

When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make command elements more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,226 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 122,601 affected page loads) ~368People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~368 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 122,601 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,697 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 122,601 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: form#Form > div.dma-full-width > p#skip-target-holder > a#skip-target

Snippet:

<a id="skip-target" name="skip-target" class="skip" tabindex="-1" innertext="Start of main content" role="button">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list)

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Snippet:

<input type="submit" value="Submit" class="btn btn-gold my-0">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.33 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #b0862c, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Accessibility Findings

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 53

Axe findings: 8

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,787 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 116,133 affected page loads) ~4KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~4,994 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 116,133 affected page loads)

Snippet:

<input type="text" id="query-field" name="inputstring" size="12" value="Local Forecast by ZIP/City, State" onfocus="this.value='';" onblur="this.value='Local Forecast by ZIP/City, State';">

`<frame>` or `<iframe>` elements do not have a title (rule: frame-title)

Screen reader users rely on frame titles to describe the contents of frames. Learn more about frame titles.

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang)

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt)

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name)

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size)

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,555 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 116,133 affected page loads) ~6KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main)

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,161 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 116,133 affected page loads) ~348People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~348 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 116,133 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,555 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 116,133 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="//www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark
`<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers. (rule: td-has-header)

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about table headers.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,161 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 116,133 affected page loads) ~348People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~348 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 116,133 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: tbody > tr > td > table

Snippet:

<table cellspacing="1" cellpadding="2" border="0" width="100%">

Label: Hazard Thu (03/26) Fri (03/27) Sat (03/28) Sun (03/29) Mon (03/30) Tue (03/31) …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Some non-empty data cells do not have table headers

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://my.navy.mil

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 85

Axe findings: 5

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr)

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,138 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 113,776 affected page loads) ~341People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~341 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 113,776 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,503 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 113,776 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: body.modal-open > div#wra-info-header > div.banner-header > span#wra-dropdown

Snippet:

<span id="wra-dropdown" data-toggle="collapse" data-target="#wra-collapse-wrapper" aria-expanded="false">

Label: Collapse here's how you know dropdown. Here's how you know ⌵︎ Dropdown

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
`<frame>` or `<iframe>` elements do not have a title (rule: frame-title)

Screen reader users rely on frame titles to describe the contents of frames. Learn more about frame titles.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,138 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 113,776 affected page loads) ~341People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~341 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 113,776 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,503 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 113,776 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: body.modal-open > iframe#embeddedMessagingSiteContextFrame

Snippet:

<iframe class="embeddedMessagingSiteContextFrame" id="embeddedMessagingSiteContextFrame" name="embeddedMessagingSiteContextFrame" src="https://navynpcremovethistoputbuttonback.experience.crmforce.mil/ESWMNCCEn…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has no title attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,731 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 113,776 affected page loads) ~4KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~4,892 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 113,776 affected page loads)

Element 1

Element path: div#alert-container > div#alert-message-box-2 > div#alert-message mnp-font > a

Label: askmncc.fct@navy.mil

  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order)

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name)

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex)

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Affected elements (1):

Element 1

Element path: div.inner > div#info-btn > a > span#arrow

Snippet:

<span id="arrow" aria-expanded="false">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast)

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,674 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 111,415 affected page loads) ~4KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~4,791 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 111,415 affected page loads)

Affected elements (5):

Element 1

Element path: body > header > div#callout-bar > p

Snippet:

<p>

Label: The change to Daylight Saving Time occurs at 2 a.m. (local time) on Sunday 03/0…

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #999999, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Element 2

Element path: div.col-25 > div.pad-2 > div.clock-box > div.title

Snippet:

<div class="title">

Label: MOUNTAIN DAYLIGHT TIME

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.86 (foreground color: #63a952, background color: #ffffff, font size: 18.0pt (24px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 3:1

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.
